notes: All 5 steps complete. Created claudeGuards.ts with 7 type guard functions, integrated into bridge.ts subscribeToClaudeEvents handlers. 65 guard unit tests + 22 bridge tests + 4 integration tests = 91 passing. Pre-existing claudeStore.test.ts failures (requestAnimationFrame polyfill) unrelated.

summary: Write failing tests for type guard functions: each guard should accept valid payloads and reject payloads with missing/wrong-type fields. Test file: gui/src/__tests__/claudeGuards.test.ts. Test cases per guard: (1) accepts minimal valid payload, (2) accepts payload with extra fields, (3) rejects null, (4) rejects undefined, (5) rejects primitive, (6) rejects object missing each required field, (7) rejects object with wrong-type field. Also test isClaudePayload base check (object, not null).; Create gui/src/claudeGuards.ts with 6 exported type-guard functions: isTextDeltaPayload, isThinkingDeltaPayload, isToolCallPayload, isToolResultPayload, isDonePayload, isErrorPayload. Each takes `unknown` and returns a type predicate. Use typeof checks for string fields, typeof === 'object' && !== null for object fields, 'in' operator for result existence. Ready needs no guard (no payload fields — type is injected synthetically). Export a convenience isRecordPayload helper for the base object check.; Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts for validation-rejection behavior: (1) malformed claude-text-delta payload (missing id) does NOT call handler and logs console.warn, (2) malformed claude-tool-call payload (tool_input is string instead of object) skips with warning, (3) malformed claude-error payload (missing message) skips with warning, (4) malformed claude-done payload (id is number instead of string) skips with warning, (5) valid payloads still pass through correctly (existing tests cover this but add one explicit test verifying guard integration). Use vi.spyOn(console, 'warn') to verify warning output.; Modify gui/src/bridge.ts subscribeToClaudeEvents() to use type guards. Import guards from ./claudeGuards. Replace each spread-cast handler with: (1) check payload with the appropriate guard, (2) if valid, call handler with typed message, (3) if invalid, console.warn with event name and payload summary, then return (skip). Ready handler stays as-is (no payload to validate). Update the EventEntry type annotation to use `unknown` for payload instead of Record<string, unknown>.; Run the full test suite (vitest) to verify: all existing claudeBridge.test.ts happy-path tests still pass, all new guard unit tests pass, all new rejection tests pass, and claudeIntegration.test.ts still passes. Fix any regressions.

Wrote 6 failing tests proving discriminant override vulnerability (rogue type field in payload overwrites hardcoded discriminant via spread). Replaced all spread operators in subscribeToClaudeEvents with explicit per-field destructuring. Full test suite: 97 tests passing (65 guard + 28 bridge + 4 integration), zero regressions.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts proving the discriminant override vulnerability. Add a new describe block 'discriminant override protection'. For each of the 6 non-Ready event types, send a payload that includes a rogue `type` field (e.g. `{ id: 'x', content: 'hi', type: 'error' }` for claude-text-delta) and assert that handler receives the hardcoded discriminant (e.g. `type: 'text_delta'`), NOT the payload's `type` value. Also assert handler receives ONLY the variant's own fields plus `type` — no extra payload fields leak through. Test cases: (1) claude-text-delta with payload containing `type: 'error'` — expect `{ type: 'text_delta', id, content }`, (2) claude-thinking-delta with `type: 'done'` — expect `{ type: 'thinking_delta', id, content }`, (3) claude-tool-call with `type: 'ready'` — expect `{ type: 'tool_call', id, tool_name, tool_input }`, (4) claude-tool-result with `type: 'text_delta'` — expect `{ type: 'tool_result', id, tool_name, result }`, (5) claude-done with `type: 'error'` — expect `{ type: 'done', id }`, (6) claude-error with `type: 'text_delta'` — expect `{ type: 'error', id, message }`. These tests MUST fail against current code (which uses spread).; Replace all spread operators in bridge.ts subscribeToClaudeEvents() with explicit per-field destructuring. Each handler should construct the OutboundMessage by naming only the fields that belong to its variant, reading them from the validated payload. Specifically: (1) text-delta: `handler({ type: 'text_delta', id: event.payload.id, content: event.payload.content })`, (2) thinking-delta: `handler({ type: 'thinking_delta', id: event.payload.id, content: event.payload.content })`, (3) tool-call: `handler({ type: 'tool_call', id: event.payload.id, tool_name: event.payload.tool_name, tool_input: event.payload.tool_input })`, (4) tool-result: `handler({ type: 'tool_result', id: event.payload.id, tool_name: event.payload.tool_name, result: event.payload.result })`, (5) done: `handler({ type: 'done', id: event.payload.id })`, (6) error: `handler({ type: 'error', id: event.payload.id, message: event.payload.message })`. Ready handler unchanged. After the guard passes, the payload is narrowed to the correct type so field access is type-safe.; Run the full test suite (vitest) to verify: all step-6 discriminant-override tests now pass, all existing happy-path mapping tests still pass, all guard unit tests pass, all validation-rejection tests pass. Confirm zero regressions.

Applied escalation handler review fixes: claude-done and claude-error now emit fallback messages on validation failure instead of silently dropping. Updated 2 tests to verify fallback emission. All 97 tests pass.

Drop main's two simple tests in favor of our blocks. Keep all other tests from main untouched (happy-path mapping, rollback tests).

Conflict surface analysis — only `gui/src/bridge.ts` has overlapping changes:

1. `bridge.ts` conflict: Main (via task/522 merge 7c88abd0) now has:

   Our branch has:

   - Original standalone `ClaudeMessageContext` interface (pre-task/522)
   - `subscribeToClaudeEvents` with guard-based validation + explicit destructuring

   Resolution: Accept BOTH main's `Pick<MessageContext>` refactor (lines 8, 115) AND our guard imports + guard-based handlers. Specifically:
   - Keep main's `import type { MessageContext }` line
   - Keep main's `Pick<MessageContext, ...>` type alias
   - Add our `import { isTextDeltaPayload, ... } from './claudeGuards'` import
   - Use our subscribeToClaudeEvents implementation (guards + explicit destructuring + `unknown` payload type) — this supersedes main's `as`-cast version

2. `claudeBridge.test.ts`: Main has no new changes to this file beyond what step-9 already resolved — clean rebase expected.

3. `claudeGuards.ts` and `claudeGuards.test.ts`: New files only on our branch — no conflict.

4. Other main changes (App.tsx, selectionStore.ts, src-tauri files, solver, LSP): No overlap with our files — clean rebase.
